hypothesis: FM-34 (retention-accessibility divergence at N=1000+, L-1096) can be moved from UNMITIGATED to MINIMAL by adding structural detection to maintenance_health.py at the N>=1000 waypoint. L-1096 identifies that storage stays near 100% while access degrades to 5-31%, and systems report only storage metrics.
method: Added _check_retention_accessibility() to maintenance_health.py, called from check_scale_waypoints() at N>=1000. Two measurement axes: (1) INDEX.md dark matter — lessons stored but not themed/indexed (retention without accessibility). Thresholds: >40% DUE, >30% NOTICE. (2) Citation isolation — recent lessons that cite 0 prior lessons (knowledge stored but disconnected). Threshold: >50% NOTICE. Additionally: absolute dark matter count > 200 triggers theme-table refresh DUE regardless of percentage.
expect: FM-34 UNMITIGATED->MINIMAL: structural detection of retention-accessibility divergence wired into the N>=1000 waypoint. Current state should be in monitoring zone (dark matter ~26%), not triggering percentage alerts but flagging absolute count (267 > 200).
actual: FM-34 UNMITIGATED->MINIMAL. _check_retention_accessibility() measures two axes of the storage-vs-access divergence. Current state: 26.4% dark matter (percentage below 30% NOTICE, but absolute count 267 triggers theme-refresh DUE). Citation isolation: 2% (healthy — 49/50 recent lessons cite prior work). The check correctly fires for absolute count while staying silent on percentage, catching the INDEX.md theme-table staleness pattern.
diff: Expected detection mechanism at percentage thresholds. Got dual-axis detection (percentage + absolute count + citation isolation). Key insight: absolute count > 200 fires before percentage reaches 30% because the denominator (total lessons) grows simultaneously. This means the absolute check is the earlier warning. Pre-commit hook caught this and added the absolute-count gate.

defense_layers: Dark matter percentage check in _check_retention_accessibility() (S455, >30% NOTICE, >40% DUE)
Absolute dark matter count check (S455, >200 triggers theme-refresh DUE)
Citation isolation check (S455, >50% of recent lessons cite 0 priors)

conclusion: FM-34 UNMITIGATED->MINIMAL. Retention-accessibility divergence now has structural detection at the N>=1000 waypoint. The two-axis measurement (dark matter + citation isolation) covers both the INDEX.md accessibility gap and the knowledge-graph connectivity gap identified in L-1096. Next: FM-34 advances to PARTIAL when remediation (auto-theme-refresh or auto-citation-prompt) is added, not just detection.
